name: iso-13485-certification
tagline: Guides medical-device makers through ISO 13485 QMS documentation: gap analysis, Quality Manual, procedure templates.
verdict: No security red flags: no credential access, no network calls, no shell-piped remote code, no manipulation attempts. Main gaps are hygiene/quality nits: SKILL.md runs ~677 lines (over the ~500 budget), the bundled gap_analyzer.py script's actual behavior is unverified since no bundled files were fetched, no negative-use guidance is given, and failure paths (

highlights.summary: gap_analyzer.py (line 45) is a bundled script that this SKILL.md-only review could not inspect for supply-chain integrity; its actual behavior is unverified from this file alone.
No 'do not use when' / negative-trigger guidance is given anywhere in the file, so an agent could misapply the skill to out-of-scope regulatory frameworks without a stated boundary.

The 'Quick Reference' section is titled 'The 31 Required Documented Procedures' but the list itself has 33 numbered entries (lines 585-621); a trailing note tries to explain the mismatch (line 621) bu
